sprint: Constellation Crossing-Lines Fix — Retroactive Repair for Already-Claimed Cards
task: "can you show me whats the pattern for vihupapa now" -- while confirming the fix, found the CONSTELLATIONS.CYGNUS reorder only helps NEW claims: MagicCard.claim() stores a card's real pattern permanently at claim time (js/magicCard.js's pattern:placed.pattern), never re-derived from the CONSTELLATIONS table on later renders, so an already-claimed CYGNUS card (like a real 'vihupapa' card claimed before the previous fix shipped) would have kept showing the old, self-crossing order forever with no way to reach it. Fixed with a real, one-time repair: a new _migrateCygnusOrder(cards) runs inside js/magicCard.js's _readCards() -- every time cards are read from localStorage, any CYGNUS card with a 5-point pattern and no constellationOrderFixed flag gets its stored points reordered via [p[0],p[1],p[3],p[2],p[4]], the exact same index permutation the base array's own Top/Center/Bottom/Left/Right -> Top/Center/Left/Bottom/Right reorder applied -- correct regardless of which random rotation/mirror the card got at claim time, since _rotate/_mirrorHorizontal/_shiftToOrigin are all plain .map() calls that never change array order (index 0 is always the transformed 'Top' point, etc, for every card). Same stars, same position, only the connect-the-dots order changes -- recall_magic_card() already matches a tapped pattern order-independently, so this can never affect whether a real device can still recall the card. Gated by the one-time flag so it runs exactly once per card and never re-touches an already-correct pattern from a claim made after the previous fix shipped (verified idempotent via two consecutive reads). Verified via a new Playwright check seeding a real pre-fix-shaped card (constellation:'CYGNUS', the old defective pattern, no fix flag, nickname 'vihupapa') and confirming: the migration runs automatically on load, the repaired pattern matches the corrected order exactly, a second reload doesn't re-touch it, and MagicCardArt.drawBack() renders the repaired card as a clean, non-overlapping kite/arrow shape (screenshotted) instead of the old 'number 4' tangle. I don't have access to the user's own real, live claimed card (Magic Cards are 100% client-side by design, stored only in the user's own browser) -- this fix means the next time they open the app in their own browser, their real card's stored pattern gets silently, automatically, and permanently repaired with zero action needed from them. Full regression: gw_decline_choice_test.js 11/11, traveller_save_notice_test.js 15/15, gw_returning_unified_test.js 18/18, and both goldenBuild.js suites (World Builder v1 and v2, unaffected -- this fix touches zero Builder files) all pass unchanged, zero console errors. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder/Creator Governing Rules change; confined to js/magicCard.js.
